name: Charles Juntikka & Associates, LLP
description_short: NYC-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 personal bankruptcy filings. Founded in 1984, claims to be the #1 filer of Chapter 7 cases in NYC for 20+ consecutive years.
description_long:
Charles Juntikka & Associates, LLP was founded on July 3, 1984, by Charles Juntikka, a University of Michigan and New York Law School graduate who serves as a member of the Association of the Bar of the City of New York. Over 32 years, the firm has grown to become the largest personal bankruptcy filer in the New York City area, with over 22,000 bankruptcies filed and a 99% approval rate. The firm explicitly positions itself as advocating for financially distressed consumers against predatory lending practices.

The firm primarily offers Chapter 7 bankruptcy filing services in New York and New Jersey, with a flat fee of $1,600 with no hidden costs and payment plans available. They provide free initial consultations both in-office (near Penn Station) and via telephone or Zoom. Beyond filing, they offer post-bankruptcy credit counseling and credit rebuilding advice to clients at no additional charge. The firm specifically highlights their ability to eliminate unsecured debts including credit card debt, bank loans, medical bills, payday loans, and utility bills.

The firm distinguishes itself through its stated track record as the #1 Chapter 7 filer in NYC for approximately 20 years running, transparent flat-fee pricing without hidden costs, and a client-advocacy philosophy centered on protecting consumers from predatory lenders. Charles Juntikka has been quoted in major publications including the New York Times and Wall Street Journal, and the firm has received coverage on CBS News and CNN regarding bankruptcy protections.

However, consumers should note that the website includes a standard disclaimer that bankruptcy may not be suitable for everyone and that each case requires individual evaluation. The firm's heavy emphasis on their filer volume and media coverage, while factually presented, should not be mistaken for a guarantee of favorable outcomes. The disclaimer also specifies that this is not legal advice and that selection of an attorney requires careful consideration.
address: 247 W 30th St 12th Floor, New York, NY 10001

company_info.headquarters: 247 W 30th St 12th Floor, New York, NY 10001

pros: Flat $1,600 fee with no hidden costs and payment plans available—transparent pricing model
Claims 22,000+ bankruptcies filed with 99% approval rate over firm history
Free post-bankruptcy credit counseling and credit rebuilding advice included with service
Multiple appointment options: in-office (near Penn Station), telephone, or Zoom consultations
Founder Charles Juntikka recognized in New York Times, Wall Street Journal, CBS News, and CNN coverage
32-year operating history with claims of being #1 Chapter 7 filer in NYC for 20+ consecutive years
Serves both New York and New Jersey clients
cons: Website disclaimer clearly states bankruptcy may not work for everyone and each case must be individually evaluated—no guarantee of debt elimination
Heavy reliance on volume metrics (22,000 filings) and media mentions could obscure individual case quality and outcomes
No specific information about attorney credentials, associate attorney profiles, or qualifications beyond the founder's background
Limited detail on the actual bankruptcy process, timeline, or what triggers the 1% of filings that are not approved
Firm explicitly identifies as a debt relief agency, which carries specific regulatory and advertising restrictions
best_for: NYC and New Jersey residents with significant unsecured debt (credit cards, medical bills, payday loans) seeking Chapter 7 discharge
Financially distressed individuals needing flat-fee bankruptcy representation without surprise legal costs
Consumers seeking post-bankruptcy credit rebuilding guidance and financial counseling alongside legal filing
diagnosis: Charles Juntikka & Associates is best suited for NYC and NJ residents with substantial unsecured debt who want transparent, flat-fee Chapter 7 bankruptcy representation with post-filing credit recovery support. The main caveat is that bankruptcy is not appropriate for all financial situations, and the firm's emphasis on filing volume should not substitute for a thorough individual case evaluation—each consumer must confirm Chapter 7 (versus Chapter 13 or other debt solutions) is genuinely optimal for their specific assets, debts, and financial goals.
services: Free initial consultation (in-office, phone, or Zoom)
Unsecured debt elimination (credit cards, bank loans, medical bills, payday loans, utility bills)
Creditor harassment cessation and legal protection
Asset protection guidance under bankruptcy law
Flat-fee bankruptcy representation ($1,600 with payment plans available)
Service in New York and New Jersey jurisdictions
